transcript.title: CBSE Class 10 Science: Chemical Reactions and Equations
concepts: Types of chemical reactions: Combination, Decomposition, Displacement, Double Displacement.
Effects of oxidation reactions in everyday life: Corrosion and Rancidity.

beats.speaker: Teacher
beats.text: Good morning, class! Today we are diving into Chemical Reactions and Equations. Why does food get spoiled, or why does iron rust? These are chemical changes.

beats.speaker: Priya - Interrupting
beats.text: Is rusting a reaction with oxygen, Teacher?

beats.speaker: Rahul - Interrupting
beats.text: Why do we have to balance chemical equations?

beats.speaker: Amit - Interrupting
beats.text: What are the main types of reactions we need to know?

beats.speaker: Sneha - Interrupting
beats.text: Ma'am, can you give an example of a combination reaction we see every day?

beats.speaker: Priya - Interrupting
beats.text: What about decomposition? Is thermal decomposition the same as electrolytic decomposition?

beats.speaker: Rahul - Interrupting
beats.text: Sir, how do we balance Fe + H₂O → Fe₃O₄ + H₂ quickly in exams?

beats.speaker: Amit - Interrupting
beats.text: You mentioned corrosion earlier — is it only about iron rusting?

beats.speaker: Priya - Interrupting
beats.text: How can we prevent corrosion of iron?

beats.speaker: Rahul - Interrupting
beats.text: What is rancidity? My mother always keeps chips in airtight jars.

beats.speaker: Sneha - Interrupting
beats.text: So both rusting and rancidity are oxidation in daily life?

pages.title: NCERT syllabus focus
bullets: Types of chemical reactions: Combination, Decomposition, Displacement, Double Displacement.

pages.title: Chapter essentials
bullets: Effects of oxidation reactions in everyday life: Corrosion and Rancidity.

chem-reactions.title: CBSE Class 10 Science: Chemical Reactions and Equations

transcript.title: CBSE Class 10 Science: Acids, Bases and Salts
concepts: Chemical properties of acids and bases (reactions with metals, carbonates).
pH scale and its importance in everyday life.
Preparation and uses of sodium hydroxide, bleaching powder, baking soda, washing soda.

beats.speaker: Teacher
beats.text: Today we talk about Acids, Bases and Salts. Acids taste sour and turn blue litmus red. Bases taste bitter, feel soapy, and turn red litmus blue.

beats.speaker: Priya - Interrupting
beats.text: How do they react with metals?

beats.speaker: Rahul - Interrupting
beats.text: What is pH?

beats.speaker: Amit - Interrupting
beats.text: What happens when acids react with metal carbonates?

beats.speaker: Sneha - Interrupting
beats.text: Ma'am, what are important salts we must know for boards?

beats.speaker: Rahul - Interrupting
beats.text: Why is soil pH important for farmers?

pages.title: NCERT syllabus focus
bullets: Chemical properties of acids and bases (reactions with metals, carbonates).
pH scale and its importance in everyday life.

pages.title: Chapter essentials
bullets: Preparation and uses of sodium hydroxide, bleaching powder, baking soda, washing soda.

acids-bases.title: CBSE Class 10 Science: Acids, Bases and Salts

transcript.title: CBSE Class 10 Science: Metals and Non-metals
concepts: Physical properties of metals and non-metals.
Reactivity series and formation of ionic compounds.
Metallurgical processes: Extraction of metals and prevention of corrosion.

beats.speaker: Teacher
beats.text: Class, let's explore Metals and Non-metals. Metals are lustrous, malleable, ductile, and good conductors of heat and electricity. Non-metals are the opposite.

beats.speaker: Amit - Interrupting
beats.text: Are there exceptions? Like mercury is liquid?

beats.speaker: Priya - Interrupting
beats.text: How do metals react with non-metals?

pages.title: NCERT syllabus focus
bullets: Physical properties of metals and non-metals.
Reactivity series and formation of ionic compounds.

pages.title: Chapter essentials
bullets: Metallurgical processes: Extraction of metals and prevention of corrosion.

metals.title: CBSE Class 10 Science: Metals and Non-metals

transcript.title: CBSE Class 10 Science: Carbon and its Compounds

beats.speaker: Teacher
beats.text: Today we study Carbon and its Compounds. Carbon has atomic number 6, meaning it has 4 valence electrons. To achieve stability, it shares electrons, forming covalent bonds.

beats.speaker: Rahul - Interrupting
beats.text: Why are there so many carbon compounds in nature?

beats.speaker: Priya - Interrupting
beats.text: What is a homologous series?

carbon.title: CBSE Class 10 Science: Carbon and its Compounds

transcript.title: CBSE Class 10 Science: Life Processes
concepts: Nutrition in plants and animals (photosynthesis, digestion).
Transportation and Excretion in humans (circulatory system, kidneys).

beats.speaker: Teacher
beats.text: Today we study Life Processes—the basic processes needed for organisms to maintain life. We will cover nutrition, respiration, transportation, and excretion.

beats.speaker: Priya - Interrupting
beats.text: What is the main difference between aerobic and anaerobic respiration?

beats.speaker: Rahul - Interrupting
beats.text: How do our kidneys filter waste?

pages.title: NCERT syllabus focus
bullets: Nutrition in plants and animals (photosynthesis, digestion).

pages.title: Chapter essentials
bullets: Transportation and Excretion in humans (circulatory system, kidneys).

transcript.title: CBSE Class 10 Science: Control and Coordination
concepts: Tropic movements in plants, plant hormones.
Control and co-ordination in animals: Nervous system, reflex action.

beats.speaker: Teacher
beats.text: Today we study Control and Coordination, which allows organisms to respond to stimuli. In animals, this is done by the nervous system and hormones. In plants, it is done solely by plant hormones or phytohormones.

beats.speaker: Sneha - Interrupting
beats.text: What are the main plant hormones we need to know?

beats.speaker: Rahul - Interrupting
beats.text: What is a reflex action?

pages.title: NCERT syllabus focus
bullets: Tropic movements in plants, plant hormones.
Control and co-ordination in animals: Nervous system, reflex action.

control.title: CBSE Class 10 Science: Control and Coordination

transcript.title: CBSE Class 10 Science: How do Organisms Reproduce?
concepts: Reproduction in plants and animals (asexual and sexual).
Reproductive health: family planning, prevention of STDs.

beats.speaker: Teacher
beats.text: Today we cover reproduction—the process by which organisms produce new individuals of their own kind. It can be asexual (involving a single parent) or sexual (involving two parents and fusion of gametes).

beats.speaker: Amit - Interrupting
beats.text: What are the common methods of asexual reproduction?

beats.speaker: Priya - Interrupting
beats.text: How does sexual reproduction occur in flowering plants?

beats.speaker: Sneha - Interrupting
beats.text: Why is reproductive health and contraception important in humans?

pages.title: NCERT syllabus focus
bullets: Reproduction in plants and animals (asexual and sexual).

pages.title: Chapter essentials
bullets: Reproductive health: family planning, prevention of STDs.

reproduction.title: CBSE Class 10 Science: How do Organisms Reproduce?

transcript.title: CBSE Class 10 Science: Heredity
concepts: Heredity; Mendel's contribution- Laws for inheritance of traits.

beats.speaker: Teacher
beats.text: Today we study Heredity—the transmission of traits from parents to offspring. Gregor Mendel, the father of genetics, conducted experiments on garden pea plants (*Pisum sativum*) and formulated the laws of inheritance.

beats.speaker: Rahul - Interrupting
beats.text: Why did Mendel choose pea plants?

beats.speaker: Priya - Interrupting
beats.text: How is sex determined in human babies?

pages.title: NCERT syllabus focus
bullets: Heredity; Mendel's contribution- Laws for inheritance of traits.

transcript.title: CBSE Class 10 Science: Light - Reflection and Refraction
concepts: Reflection of light by curved surfaces; Images formed by spherical mirrors.
Refraction; Laws of refraction, refractive index.
Refraction of light by spherical lens; Images formed by spherical lenses; Lens formula and magnification; Power of a lens.

beats.speaker: Teacher
beats.text: Class, let's explore Light. We cover reflection by spherical mirrors and refraction by lenses.

beats.speaker: Amit - Interrupting
beats.text: What is the difference between real and virtual images?

beats.speaker: Priya - Interrupting
beats.text: What about lenses? Is the formula different?

beats.speaker: Rahul - Interrupting
beats.text: What is the power of a lens?

pages.title: NCERT syllabus focus
bullets: Reflection of light by curved surfaces; Images formed by spherical mirrors.

pages.title: Chapter essentials
bullets: Refraction; Laws of refraction, refractive index.
Refraction of light by spherical lens; Images formed by spherical lenses; Lens formula and magnification; Power of a lens.

light.title: CBSE Class 10 Science: Light - Reflection and Refraction

transcript.title: CBSE Class 10 Science: The Human Eye and the Colorful World
concepts: Chapter**: The Human Eye and the Colorful World
Functioning of a lens in human eye, defects of vision and their corrections.
Refraction of light through a prism, dispersion of light, scattering of light.

beats.speaker: Teacher
beats.text: Today we study the Human Eye and natural optical phenomena. The eye lens adjusts its focal length to focus objects at different distances on the retina—a property called accommodation.

beats.speaker: Sneha - Interrupting
beats.text: What are the main vision defects?

beats.speaker: Amit - Interrupting
beats.text: Why does a prism split white light into a rainbow of colors?

beats.speaker: Priya - Interrupting
beats.text: Why is the sky blue?

pages.title: NCERT syllabus focus
bullets: Chapter**: The Human Eye and the Colorful World
Functioning of a lens in human eye, defects of vision and their corrections.
Refraction of light through a prism, dispersion of light, scattering of light.

human-eye.title: CBSE Class 10 Science: The Human Eye and the Colorful World

transcript.title: CBSE Class 10 Science: Electricity
concepts: Electric current, potential difference and electric current.
Ohm's law; Resistance, Resistivity, factors on which resistance depends.
Series and parallel combination of resistors.
Heating effect of electric current and its applications in daily life; Electric power.

beats.speaker: Teacher
beats.text: Today we study Electricity. Electric current $I$ is the flow of charge: $I = Q/t$. Potential difference $V$ is the work done to move a unit charge: $V = W/Q$. These are related by Ohm's Law: $V = IR$ at constant temperature.

beats.speaker: Rahul - Interrupting
beats.text: What determines the resistance $R$ of a wire?

beats.speaker: Priya - Interrupting
beats.text: How do we combine resistors?

beats.speaker: Amit - Interrupting
beats.text: What is the heating effect of current?

pages.title: NCERT syllabus focus
bullets: Electric current, potential difference and electric current.
Ohm's law; Resistance, Resistivity, factors on which resistance depends.

pages.title: Chapter essentials
bullets: Series and parallel combination of resistors.
Heating effect of electric current and its applications in daily life; Electric power.

transcript.title: CBSE Class 10 Science: Magnetic Effects of Electric Current
concepts: Chapter**: Magnetic Effects of Electric Current
Magnetic field, field lines, field due to a current carrying conductor.
Force on current carrying conductor, Fleming's Left Hand Rule.
Direct current. Alternating current: frequency of AC. Advantage of AC over DC.

beats.speaker: Teacher
beats.text: Class, let's explore the link between electricity and magnetism. Oersted discovered that a current-carrying wire produces a magnetic field around it.

beats.speaker: Rahul - Interrupting
beats.text: How do we find the direction of this magnetic field?

beats.speaker: Priya - Interrupting
beats.text: What happens if we place a current-carrying wire inside a magnetic field?

beats.speaker: Sneha - Interrupting
beats.text: Why do we use Alternating Current (AC) in our homes instead of Direct Current (DC)?

pages.title: NCERT syllabus focus
bullets: Chapter**: Magnetic Effects of Electric Current
Magnetic field, field lines, field due to a current carrying conductor.
Force on current carrying conductor, Fleming's Left Hand Rule.

pages.title: Chapter essentials
bullets: Direct current. Alternating current: frequency of AC. Advantage of AC over DC.

magnetism.title: CBSE Class 10 Science: Magnetic Effects of Electric Current

transcript.title: CBSE Class 10 Science: Our Environment
concepts: Eco-system, Environmental problems, Ozone depletion, waste production and their solutions.

beats.speaker: Teacher
beats.text: Today we discuss Our Environment, focusing on ecosystems. An ecosystem consists of biotic (living) and abiotic (non-living) components. Energy flows through food chains: producers $\to$ herbivores $\to$ carnivores.

beats.speaker: Sneha - Interrupting
beats.text: Why is the energy transfer between trophic levels so low?

beats.speaker: Amit - Interrupting
beats.text: What is ozone depletion, and why is it dangerous?

pages.title: NCERT syllabus focus
bullets: Eco-system, Environmental problems, Ozone depletion, waste production and their solutions.

transcript.title: CBSE Class 10 Mathematics: Real Numbers
concepts: Fundamental Theorem of Arithmetic - statement and illustration.
Rational numbers and decimal expansions (terminating and non-terminating repeating).

beats.speaker: Teacher
beats.text: Hello class! Today, we are opening the door to Chapter 1 of Class 10 Mathematics: Real Numbers. Let's start with a simple question: what makes a number 'real'? Amit, what do you think?

beats.speaker: Amit - Interrupting
beats.text: Is it any number we can write on a number line, Teacher? Like integers and fractions?

beats.speaker: Priya - Interrupting
beats.text: Teacher, what about irrational numbers like √2? How do we prove they are irrational?

beats.speaker: Rahul - Interrupting
beats.text: Oh, so because the assumption leads to a logical failure, the opposite must be true?

pages.title: NCERT syllabus focus
bullets: Fundamental Theorem of Arithmetic - statement and illustration.

pages.title: Chapter essentials
bullets: Rational numbers and decimal expansions (terminating and non-terminating repeating).

transcript.title: CBSE Class 10 Mathematics: Polynomials
concepts: Geometrical meaning of the zeroes of a polynomial.
Relationship between zeroes and coefficients of a quadratic polynomial.

beats.speaker: Teacher
beats.text: Welcome back, class. Today we are studying Polynomials. A polynomial is an algebraic expression consisting of variables and coefficients. The highest power of the variable is called the degree of the polynomial.

beats.speaker: Rahul - Interrupting
beats.text: So, a quadratic polynomial always has a degree of 2, right?

beats.speaker: Priya - Interrupting
beats.text: How do we find the relation between these zeroes and the coefficients a, b, and c?

pages.title: NCERT syllabus focus
bullets: Geometrical meaning of the zeroes of a polynomial.
Relationship between zeroes and coefficients of a quadratic polynomial.

transcript.title: CBSE Class 10 Mathematics: Pair of Linear Equations in Two Variables
concepts: Chapter**: Pair of Linear Equations in Two Variables
Graphical method of solution of a pair of linear equations.
Algebraic methods: Substitution, Elimination, and Cross-multiplication.
Consistency and inconsistency conditions (a1/a2, b1/b2, c1/c2).

beats.speaker: Teacher
beats.text: Today we are discussing systems of linear equations in two variables, represented as a₁x + b₁y + c₁ = 0 and a₂x + b₂y + c₂ = 0.

beats.speaker: Amit - Interrupting
beats.text: How do we know if they have a solution without drawing them?

beats.speaker: Priya - Interrupting
beats.text: Which algebraic method is the most efficient to solve them? Substitution or Elimination?

pages.title: NCERT syllabus focus
bullets: Chapter**: Pair of Linear Equations in Two Variables
Graphical method of solution of a pair of linear equations.
Algebraic methods: Substitution, Elimination, and Cross-multiplication.

pages.title: Chapter essentials
bullets: Consistency and inconsistency conditions (a1/a2, b1/b2, c1/c2).

linear-eq.title: CBSE Class 10 Mathematics: Pair of Linear Equations in Two Variables

transcript.title: CBSE Class 10 Mathematics: Quadratic Equations
concepts: Standard form of a quadratic equation ax2 + bx + c = 0.
Solutions by factorization, and by using the quadratic formula.
Relationship between discriminant and nature of roots.

beats.speaker: Teacher
beats.text: Today we cover Quadratic Equations. The standard form is ax² + bx + c = 0. To solve it, we can factorize it by splitting the middle term, or we can use the universal quadratic formula: x = [-b ± √(b² - 4ac)] / 2a.

beats.speaker: Rahul - Interrupting
beats.text: What is that term under the square root, b² - 4ac?

beats.speaker: Priya - Interrupting
beats.text: So if D is negative, we can't solve it in real numbers?

pages.title: NCERT syllabus focus
bullets: Standard form of a quadratic equation ax2 + bx + c = 0.
Solutions by factorization, and by using the quadratic formula.

pages.title: Chapter essentials
bullets: Relationship between discriminant and nature of roots.

transcript.title: CBSE Class 10 Mathematics: Arithmetic Progressions
concepts: Derivation of the nth term of an AP.
Sum of the first n terms of an AP formula.
Real-life applications of AP in daily life.

beats.speaker: Teacher
beats.text: Class, let's talk about patterns. An Arithmetic Progression, or AP, is a sequence of numbers in which the difference between consecutive terms is constant. We call this the common difference, 'd'.

beats.speaker: Amit - Interrupting
beats.text: Can 'd' be negative?

beats.speaker: Priya - Interrupting
beats.text: How do we find the sum of all terms in an AP?

pages.title: NCERT syllabus focus
bullets: Derivation of the nth term of an AP.
Sum of the first n terms of an AP formula.

pages.title: Chapter essentials
bullets: Real-life applications of AP in daily life.

transcript.title: CBSE Class 10 Mathematics: Triangles
concepts: Definitions, examples, counter examples of similar triangles.
Basic Proportionality Theorem (Thales Theorem) and its proof.
Criteria for similarity of triangles: AAA, SSS, and SAS.

beats.speaker: Teacher
beats.text: Today we study Triangles, specifically similar triangles. Two triangles are similar if their corresponding angles are equal and their corresponding sides are in the same ratio.

beats.speaker: Rahul - Interrupting
beats.text: Is similarity different from congruence?

beats.speaker: Sneha - Interrupting
beats.text: How do we prove this theorem?

beats.speaker: Amit - Interrupting
beats.text: What are the similarity criteria?

pages.title: NCERT syllabus focus
bullets: Definitions, examples, counter examples of similar triangles.
Basic Proportionality Theorem (Thales Theorem) and its proof.

pages.title: Chapter essentials
bullets: Criteria for similarity of triangles: AAA, SSS, and SAS.

transcript.title: CBSE Class 10 Mathematics: Coordinate Geometry
concepts: Review: Concepts of coordinate geometry, graphs of linear equations.
Section formula (internal division) and midpoint formula.

beats.speaker: Teacher
beats.text: Today we discuss Coordinate Geometry, which links algebra and geometry. Any point on the Cartesian plane is represented by coordinates $(x, y)$.

beats.speaker: Priya - Interrupting
beats.text: How do we calculate the distance between two points, say $(x_1, y_1)$ and $(x_2, y_2)$?

beats.speaker: Amit - Interrupting
beats.text: What if we want to find a point that divides a line segment in a specific ratio?

beats.speaker: Rahul - Interrupting
beats.text: What if $P$ is the exact midpoint?

pages.title: NCERT syllabus focus
bullets: Review: Concepts of coordinate geometry, graphs of linear equations.

pages.title: Chapter essentials
bullets: Section formula (internal division) and midpoint formula.

transcript.title: CBSE Class 10 Mathematics: Introduction to Trigonometry
concepts: Trigonometric ratios of an acute angle of a right-angled triangle.
Values of the trigonometric ratios of 30, 45, 60, 90 degrees.
Relationships between the ratios and trigonometric identities.

beats.speaker: Teacher
beats.text: Today we introduce Trigonometry—the study of relationships between sides and angles of triangles. Let's look at a right-angled triangle. We define six ratios relative to an acute angle $\theta$: $\sin \theta = \text{Opposite}/\text{Hypotenuse}$, $\cos \theta = \text{Adjacent}/\text{Hypotenuse}$, and $\tan \theta = \text{Opposite}/\text{Adjacent}$.

beats.speaker: Rahul - Interrupting
beats.text: Are the other three ratios just the reciprocals?

beats.speaker: Priya - Interrupting
beats.text: What are the key values we must memorize?

beats.speaker: Amit - Interrupting
beats.text: What is the most important identity in trigonometry?

pages.title: NCERT syllabus focus
bullets: Trigonometric ratios of an acute angle of a right-angled triangle.
Values of the trigonometric ratios of 30, 45, 60, 90 degrees.

pages.title: Chapter essentials
bullets: Relationships between the ratios and trigonometric identities.

trigonometry.title: CBSE Class 10 Mathematics: Introduction to Trigonometry

transcript.title: CBSE Class 10 Mathematics: Some Applications of Trigonometry
concepts: Simple problems on heights and distances.
Angles of elevation and depression (30, 45, 60 degrees).

beats.speaker: Teacher
beats.text: Today we study Heights and Distances, applying trigonometry to real life, like finding the height of a tower or tree without measuring it directly. We define two key angles: 1. Angle of Elevation: The angle formed by the line of sight with the horizontal when looking up. 2. Angle of Depression: The angle formed by the line of sight when looking down.

beats.speaker: Sneha - Interrupting
beats.text: How do we solve these problems? Which ratio do we use most?

beats.speaker: Rahul - Interrupting
beats.text: If we look down from a lighthouse, is the angle of depression equal to the angle of elevation from the boat?

pages.title: NCERT syllabus focus
bullets: Simple problems on heights and distances.
Angles of elevation and depression (30, 45, 60 degrees).

trig-apps.title: CBSE Class 10 Mathematics: Some Applications of Trigonometry

transcript.title: CBSE Class 10 Mathematics: Circles
concepts: Tangent to a circle at point of contact.
Theorem: The tangent at any point of a circle is perpendicular to the radius.
Theorem: The lengths of tangents drawn from an external point to a circle are equal.

beats.speaker: Teacher
beats.text: Today we study Circles, focusing on tangents. A tangent is a line that intersects a circle at exactly one point, called the point of contact.

beats.speaker: Amit - Interrupting
beats.text: Can we draw multiple tangents from a single point?

beats.speaker: Priya - Interrupting
beats.text: What about the lengths of the two tangents drawn from an external point?

pages.title: NCERT syllabus focus
bullets: Tangent to a circle at point of contact.
Theorem: The tangent at any point of a circle is perpendicular to the radius.

pages.title: Chapter essentials
bullets: Theorem: The lengths of tangents drawn from an external point to a circle are equal.

transcript.title: CBSE Class 10 Mathematics: Areas Related to Circles
concepts: Area of a sector and segment of a circle.
Problems based on areas and perimeter / circumference of circles.

beats.speaker: Teacher
beats.text: Today we calculate areas related to parts of a circle—specifically, sectors and segments. A sector is the region bounded by two radii and an arc. The area of a sector with angle $\theta$ is $\text{Area} = \frac{\theta}{360^\circ} \times \pi r^2$.

beats.speaker: Sneha - Interrupting
beats.text: What about a segment of a circle?

beats.speaker: Rahul - Interrupting
beats.text: How do we find the area of $\Delta OAB$ if the angle $\theta$ is $120^\circ$?

pages.title: NCERT syllabus focus
bullets: Area of a sector and segment of a circle.
Problems based on areas and perimeter / circumference of circles.

areas-circles.title: CBSE Class 10 Mathematics: Areas Related to Circles

transcript.title: CBSE Class 10 Mathematics: Surface Areas and Volumes
concepts: Surface areas and volumes of combinations of any two of: cubes, cuboids, spheres, hemispheres, right circular cylinders/cones.

beats.speaker: Teacher
beats.text: Class, let's study Surface Areas and Volumes of solid shapes. In Class 9 we learned about individual shapes. In Class 10 we study combinations of these shapes, like a toy made of a cone on a hemisphere.

beats.speaker: Amit - Interrupting
beats.text: When combining solids, do we add their surface areas directly?

beats.speaker: Priya - Interrupting
beats.text: What about the total volume of combined solids? Is that added directly?

pages.title: NCERT syllabus focus
bullets: Surface areas and volumes of combinations of any two of: cubes, cuboids, spheres, hemispheres, right circular cylinders/cones.

surface-vol.title: CBSE Class 10 Mathematics: Surface Areas and Volumes

transcript.title: CBSE Class 10 Mathematics: Statistics
concepts: Mean, median and mode of grouped data.
Cumulative frequency graph (ogive) - brief conceptual review.

beats.speaker: Teacher
beats.text: Today we cover Statistics. We will calculate the measures of central tendency for grouped data: Mean, Median, and Mode.

beats.speaker: Rahul - Interrupting
beats.text: Which formula do we use for the Mean of grouped data?

beats.speaker: Sneha - Interrupting
beats.text: How do we find the Mode of grouped data?

beats.speaker: Priya - Interrupting
beats.text: Is there a relation between mean, median, and mode?

pages.title: NCERT syllabus focus
bullets: Mean, median and mode of grouped data.
Cumulative frequency graph (ogive) - brief conceptual review.

transcript.title: CBSE Class 10 Mathematics: Probability
concepts: Simple problems on finding the probability of an event.

beats.speaker: Teacher
beats.text: Today we study Probability—the measure of likelihood that an event will occur. The classical probability of an event $E$ is $P(E) = \frac{\text{Number of outcomes favorable to } E}{\text{Total number of possible outcomes}}$.

beats.speaker: Amit - Interrupting
beats.text: What is the range of probability values? Can it be greater than 1?

beats.speaker: Priya - Interrupting
beats.text: What are complementary events?

pages.title: NCERT syllabus focus
bullets: Simple problems on finding the probability of an event.
